idea.hook: What if a *star* was smaller than your city?
facts: The average diameter of a major city, like London or New York, is approximately 50 to 100 kilometers.
A neutron star, the super-dense remnant of a giant star, typically measures only about 20 to 25 kilometers across.
Yet, this tiny stellar core contains 1.4 to 2.1 times the mass of our Sun!
idea.payoff: Imagine an entire star, weighing more than the Sun, fitting within the boundaries of a metropolitan area! Density beyond comprehension.
idea.title: Star Smaller Than a City?! 🤯🏙️

scenes.text: What if a STAR was smaller than your CITY?
scenes.visual: Dark cosmic background with a faint, silhouetted city skyline appearing from the bottom. Text fades in over this.
Animated overhead satellite map view of a major city (e.g., London or New York), with a dynamic measuring line extending across, showing '50-100 km'.
Cosmic background (space_dark). A small, super-dense, glowing neutron star sphere appears, with a measuring line next to it indicating '20-25 km'.

scenes.text: But packs 1.4 - 2.1x the mass of our Sun!
scenes.visual: The neutron star remains, now glowing more intensely. An iconic representation of our Sun appears next to it, with a graphic showing '1.4x - 2.1x' mass comparison.

scenes.text: A STAR heavier than the Sun... FITS IN YOUR CITY! Density beyond comprehension.
scenes.visual: The overhead city map reappears. The tiny, glowing neutron star graphic is superimposed dramatically *within* the city's boundaries, clearly showing its minuscule size relative to the city. Camera slightly pulls back to show the full scale.
